title: Euclidean gcd via genuine well-founded recursion
informal_statement: Define myGcd by the Euclidean algorithm (myGcd a 0 = a; myGcd a (b+1) = myGcd (b+1) (a % (b+1))) via genuine well-founded recursion on the second argument (a % (b+1) < b+1, not a direct predecessor). Prove the base-case unfolding fact myGcd a 0 = a.
informal_proof_idea: myGcd is defined via Nat.strongRecOn with motive (fun _ => Nat -> Nat), threading the first gcd argument through the step function and decreasing on the second argument via Nat.mod_lt. Since Nat.strongRecOn (built on WellFounded.fix) does not reduce definitionally, the base-case identity is proved via simp using the Nat.strongRecOn_eq unfolding lemma rather than rfl.
formal_statement_pp: theorem mygcd_wellfounded (a : ℕ) : myGcd a 0 = a

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 7e36dbd3), kernel_verified on the third submit_module attempt. Step 1: ordinary equation-compiler pattern syntax for myGcd kernel-failed with 'Could not find a decreasing measure' / 'Please use termination_by' -- confirming SubmitModule's expression-only def items cannot express genuinely well-founded recursion via the equation compiler's automatic termination checker. Step 2: switched to an explicit Nat.strongRecOn term, which compiled, but the base-case rfl failed since Nat.strongRecOn (built on WellFounded.fix) is not definitionally reducible. Step 3: replaced rfl with simp [myGcd, Nat.strongRecOn_eq], which closed it. Closes the 'well-founded (non-structural) recursion via SubmitModule' backlog item in packets/elementary/induction/QUEUE.md -- every LOOP.md-listed recursion technique (structural, mutual, well-founded) is now demonstrated in this domain.
